# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export async function middleware(req) {
  const token = req.cookies.get('auth_token');

  if (!token) {
    // Redirect to login if no token is found
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }

  try {
    // Verify the token (implement your token verification logic here)
    const user = await verifyToken(token);

    if (!user) {
      // Redirect to login if token is invalid
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
    }

    // Token is valid, allow the request to proceed
    return NextResponse.next();
  } catch (error) {
    // Handle token verification errors
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }
}

async function verifyToken(token) {
  // Implement your token verification logic here (e.g., JWT verification)
  // Return user data if valid, or null/undefined if invalid
  return token === 'valid_token' ? { id: 1, name: 'User' } : null;
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected/*'], // Apply middleware to specific routes
};
